articles.title: Oil Jumps Over $6 On Iran Report Of Halted U.S.-Tehran Exchanges, Hormuz Blockade Risk
articles.content:
纽约，6月1日（路透社）- 周一，油价每桶飙升超过6美元，此前伊朗塔斯尼姆通讯社报道称，德黑兰谈判团队已停止与美国的信息交流，其“抵抗阵线”正在考虑采取措施完全封锁霍尔木兹海峡，并扼制包括巴布厄尔-曼德海峡在内的其他水道。

该报道发布之际，伊朗与美国相互发动袭击，以色列也下令军队进一步进入黎巴嫩，以对抗受德黑兰支持的真主党武装组织。

周一上午10点02分（东部时间），布伦特原油期货上涨6.02美元或6.6%，至每桶97.14美元，而美国原油期货上涨6.68美元或7.7%，至每桶94.04美元。

整个5月，布伦特原油和西德克萨斯中质原油（WTI）分别下跌约19%和17%，这是自2020年3月新冠疫情导致能源需求骤降以来，两种合约在绝对数值上的最大月度跌幅。

周一，全球股市在接近历史高点的水平保持稳定，人工智能热潮继续推动需求。石油通常与其他风险资产如股票同步交易。

在美国周五主办以色列-黎巴嫩和平谈判后，中东地区的冲突使美国和伊朗可能很快宣布延长停火协议的希望破灭。

美国总统唐纳德·特朗普周五表示，他将很快对一项延长4月初宣布的停火协议的提议做出决定。

以色列将是任何此类协议的关键，而伊朗已多次表示，必须包括真主党和黎巴嫩。一位美国官员周日表示，美国提出了“逐步降级”计划。

IG分析师托尼·西卡莫尔在一份报告中表示，对霍尔木兹海峡布设水雷的担忧正在增加，该海峡是关键的石油和天然气运输通道。

“即使达成协议，也不会带来供应的激增，”西卡莫尔表示。

Axios周五在社交平台X上报道称，伊朗上周在该海峡投放了更多水雷。

伊朗外交部发言人埃斯梅尔·巴盖周一表示，外交进程拖延以结束战争，可以归因于缺乏信任、美国立场矛盾以及以色列对黎巴嫩的袭击。

对供应的担忧超过了周末中国发布的经济数据，这些数据显示工厂活动陷入停滞。这加剧了对世界第二大经济体正在失去增长势头的担忧。

路透社的一项调查显示，沙特阿拉伯可能会连续第二个月下调7月份对亚洲的原油官方销售价格（OSPs）。

据RBC新闻网站周一报道，两名知情人士透露，俄罗斯政府打算增加从白俄罗斯供应的燃料，并加强对汽油和柴油出口的监管，以满足国内燃料需求。

该报道称，包括在某些政府间协议下，正在讨论对汽油出口实施为期两个月的全面禁令。

哈萨克斯坦能源部长埃尔兰·阿肯日诺夫周一表示，哈萨克斯坦已将石油日产量恢复至29万吨，此前该国最大油田田吉兹油田曾出现生产损失。

高盛周日表示，中国和欧洲疲软的石油需求对其第四季度布伦特原油每桶90美元和WTI每桶83美元的预测构成重大下行风险，尽管中东供应中断仍可能推高油价。

（记者：西达尔特·卡瓦莱、沙迪亚·纳斯拉拉；补充报道：科琳·豪、萨姆·李；编辑：杰米·弗里德、埃米莉亚·西托利斯、苏珊·芬顿和尼克·齐明斯基）
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

NEW YORK, June 1 (Reuters) - Oil prices surged more than $6 per barrel on Monday after Iran’s Tasnim news agency reported that Tehran’s negotiating team has halted message exchanges with the United States and its allied “Resistance Front” are considering measures to completely block the Strait of Hormuz and choking other waterways including the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.

The report comes after Iran and the U.S. traded strikes and Israel ordered troops to move further into Lebanon in its battle with the Tehran-backed Hezbollah militant group.

Advertisement

Brent futures rose $6.02 or 6.6% at $97.14 a barrel at 10.02 a.m. ET while U.S. crude futures rose $6.68 or 7.7% to $94.04 a barrel.

Over May, Brent and WTI lost around 19% and 17%, respectively. It was both contracts’ biggest monthly fall in absolute terms since March 2020 when the COVID-19 pandemic slashed energy demand.

Global equities held steady near record highs on Monday as the AI boom continued to drive demand. Oil often trades in tandem with other risk assets such as equities.

Advertisement

The fighting in the Middle East, after Washington hosted Israel-Lebanon peace talks on Friday, dimmed hopes that the U.S. and Iran could soon announce an extension to their ceasefire.

U.S. President Donald Trump said on Friday he would soon decide on a proposed deal to extend a ceasefire announced in early April.

Israel would be key to any such deal, and Iran has said repeatedly that Hezbollah and Lebanon must be included. The U.S. has proposed a “gradual de-escalation” plan, a U.S. official said on Sunday.

Advertisement

Concerns are rising about mines in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil and gas shipping lane, IG analyst Tony Sycamore said in a note.

“Even if an agreement is reached, it won’t deliver a flood of supply,” Sycamore said.

An Axios report said on X on Friday that Iran had dropped more mines in the strait last week.

Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said on Monday the delay in the diplomatic process to end the war can be explained by a lack of trust, Washington’s contradictory positions and Israel’s attacks on Lebanon.

Advertisement

Concerns over supply outweighed weekend economic data from China which showed stalling factory activity. This added to concerns the world’s second-largest economy is losing momentum.

Saudi Arabia is likely to cut its official selling prices (OSPs) for crude oil to Asia in July for a second month, a Reuters survey showed.

The Russian government intends to increase fuel supplies from Belarus and tighten oversight over exports of gasoline and diesel to meet domestic fuel demand, the RBC news outlet reported on Monday, citing two sources familiar with the matter.

Advertisement

A complete ban on gasoline exports for two months, including under some inter-governmental agreements, is under discussion, the report said.

Kazakhstan has restored its oil production to 290,000 metric tons per day following earlier production losses at the country’s largest oilfield, Tengiz, Energy Minister Erlan Akkenzhenov said on Monday.

Goldman Sachs said on Sunday that weak oil demand in China and Europe poses a major downside risk to its fourth-quarter Brent crude forecast of $90 a barrel and WTI forecast of $83, although Middle East supply disruptions could still push prices higher.

Advertisement

(Reporting by Siddharth Cavale and Shadia Nasralla, Additional reporting by Colleen Howe and Sam Li; Editing by Jamie Freed, Emelia Sithole-Matarise, Susan Fenton and Nick Zieminski)

articles.title: Israel Orders Strikes On Beirut’s Southern Suburbs As Hezbollah Fires Rockets
articles.content:
贝鲁特（美联社）——以色列政府周一下令对贝鲁特南部郊区进行空袭，此前一天以色列地面部队推进至黎巴嫩境内26年来的最深位置，而黎巴嫩真主党则向包括海法市郊在内的以色列北部地区发射了火箭弹。

以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡和国防部长以色列·卡茨发表联合声明称，由于真主党反复违反停火协议并对“我们的城市和公民发动袭击”，他们已下令以色列军队打击贝鲁特南部郊区达希耶的目标。达希耶在阿拉伯语中意为“南部郊区”。

真主党曾在四月中旬达成停火协议后暂停对以色列的攻击，但在以色列以自卫为由对黎巴嫩发动袭击后恢复了行动。

周一发出警告后，大量民众被看到正从达希耶撤离，通往郊区的道路出现拥堵，该地区是真主党的重要支持基地。

以色列与真主党夜间交火

周一夜间的以色列空袭造成黎巴嫩南部六人死亡，其中包括靠近纳巴蒂亚市的一个村庄中的一名叙利亚公民，国家通讯社报道。

与此同时，以色列军方表示空军拦截了从黎巴嫩发射的两枚炮弹以及一个可疑空中目标，该目标出现在以色列军队在黎巴嫩南部行动的区域。军方称未有人员受伤。

真主党表示周日对以色列北部进行了火箭弹和导弹袭击。

以色列与黎巴嫩即将在华盛顿举行会谈

尽管自4月17日以来名义上的停火协议一直有效，但最新袭击仍在此后发生，就在黎巴嫩和以色列即将于周二开始在华盛顿举行下一轮直接会谈之前。真主党拒绝参加直接谈判，寄望于伊朗在与华盛顿的谈判中施压，要求结束黎巴嫩战争。

以色列和黎巴嫩高级官员四月在华盛顿开始谈判，这是两国三十多年来首次直接对话，尽管双方没有正式外交关系。

自停火协议生效以来，贝鲁特大部分地区免受空袭，除了五月两次针对该市南部郊区的定点打击。

一位美国官员周日晚些时候表示，国务卿马可·鲁比奥已致电黎巴嫩总统米歇尔·奥恩和以色列总理内塔尼亚胡，提出继续进行中的谈判的新路径，同时唐纳德·特朗普总统正在考虑与伊朗就暂时延长停火协议进行谈判。

根据提案，真主党将停止对以色列的所有攻击，而以色列将避免在黎巴嫩首都贝鲁特升级军事行动，该官员在匿名讨论私人外交对话时透露。

该官员表示奥恩对这一想法持开放态度，但黎巴嫩议会主席纳比赫·贝里回应称以色列必须首先停止所有军事行动。

该官员表示特朗普政府不指望以色列放弃对真主党在其领土袭击的报复行动。

贝里的关键真主党盟友周日发表声明称，他可以保证该武装组织"全面、完整和立即遵守停火协议"。贝里补充道："但谁来迫使以色列停止侵略？"

奥恩周一在办公室发表的评论中表示："黎巴嫩正面临激烈而应受谴责的以色列侵略。"奥恩补充说，他的政府正在继续努力结束"黎巴嫩人民，特别是南部居民的苦难"。

以色列与真主党之间最新一轮冲突已造成黎巴嫩3412人死亡，超过100万人流离失所。

以色列军方表示，一名士兵在黎巴嫩南部的无人机袭击中丧生。真主党使用难以探测的光纤无人机对以色列军队造成了致命打击，以色列军队正努力应对。

据内塔尼亚胡办公室称，至少26名以色列士兵和一名国防承包商在黎巴嫩南部或附近地区丧生。另有两名平民在以色列北部丧生。

____

梅尔泽从以色列特拉维夫报道。美联社记者马修·李从华盛顿为本报道做出贡献。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

BEIRUT (AP) — Israel’s government ordered strikes on the southern suburbs of Beirut on Monday, a day after its ground forces reached their deepest point in Lebanon in 26 years and as Hezbollah fired rockets at northern Israel including the outskirts of coastal city of Haifa.

A joint statement by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Defense Minister Israel Katz said that following what they called repeated violations of the ceasefire by Hezbollah and the “attacks against our cities and citizens,” they have ordered the Israeli military to attack targets in Beirut’s southern suburbs known in Arabic as Dahiyeh.

Advertisement

Hezbollah agreed to halt attacks on Israel when the ceasefire was signed in mid-April but resumed following Israeli strikes in Lebanon that Israel characterized as self-defense.

After Monday’s warning, large numbers of people were seen fleeing Dahiyeh, jamming roads leading out of the suburb, where Hezbollah enjoys wide support.

Advertisement

Israel And Hezbollah Exchange Fire Overnight

Israeli airstrikes overnight on southern Lebanon left six people dead, including a Syrian citizen in a village near the city of Nabatiyeh, the state-run National News Agency said.

The Israeli military meanwhile said the Air Force had intercepted two projectiles launched from Lebanon toward Israeli territory, as well as a suspicious aerial target in the area where Israeli soldiers are operating in southern Lebanon. No injuries were reported, the military said.

Hezbollah said it carried out rocket and missile attacks on northern Israel on Sunday.

Israel And Lebanon Set To Hold Talks In Washington

The latest attacks came despite a nominal ceasefire that has been in place since April 17 and just before Lebanon and Israel hold their next round of direct talks in Washington starting Tuesday. Hezbollah has rejected direct talks, counting on pressure from Iran, which has demanded an end to the war in Lebanon in its talks with Washington.

Advertisement

Talks between senior officials from Israel and Lebanon began in April in Washington, the first in more than three decades between the countries, which have no formal diplomatic relations.

Beirut has been mostly spared from airstrikes since the ceasefire went into effect, apart from two targeted attacks on the city’s southern suburbs in May.

A U.S. official said late Sunday that Secretary of State Marco Rubio had spoken to Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to propose a fresh path to continue ongoing negotiations, as President Donald Trump weighs a tentative ceasefire extension with Iran.

Advertisement

Under the proposal, Hezbollah would halt all attacks on Israel and Israel would refrain from escalating military operations in the Lebanese capital of Beirut, according to the official who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss private diplomatic conversations.

The official said Aoun was open to the idea but that Lebanese parliament chief Nabih Berri had responded by demanding that Israel first stop all military action.

The official said the Trump administration does not expect Israel to give up retaliating for Hezbollah strikes on its territory.

Advertisement

Berri, a key Hezbollah ally, said in a statement Sunday that he can guarantee the militant group’s “full, comprehensive and immediate commitment to a ceasefire.” Berri added “but who will force Israel to stop its aggression?”

Aoun on Monday said in comments released by his office that “Lebanon is facing a fierce and condemned Israeli aggression.” Aoun added that his government is continuing work to end “the suffering of the Lebanese in general and the southerners in particular.”

The latest round of fighting between Israel and Hezbollah has killed 3,412 people in Lebanon and displaced more than 1 million people.

Advertisement

Israel’s military said a soldier was killed in southern Lebanon overnight in a drone attack by Hezbollah. Hezbollah’s use of hard-to-detect fiber optic drones has been deadly for the Israeli military, which is struggling to respond.

According to Netanyahu’s office, at least 26 Israeli soldiers and a defense contractor have been killed in or near southern Lebanon. Two civilians have also been killed in northern Israel.

____

Melzer reported from Tel Aviv, Israel. Associated Press writer Matthew Lee contributed to this report from Washington.

Advertisement
